mascotLines: A squirrel ran past while I tested the Pending Activation export. It got away. The export got all 30-plus rows. I call that a win.
Password updates were failing everywhere for a bit. I would never forget my own password. I'm a very good dog.
Fixed a bug where documents got stuck saying 'Sending' forever — turns out even I don't wait that patiently for a treat.
CRM import now warns you before things go wrong. Unlike me, before I dig a hole in the yard.

items.text: Manage & charge payment methods on behalf of the client
items.desc: Firms can now manage and charge a client's saved payment method directly, without needing the client to do it themselves.

items.text: Auto-move now runs for Completed jobs
items.desc: Auto-move used to only apply to active jobs — now it also runs for jobs marked Completed.
The Clients page should load noticeably faster now.
You can now schedule a bulk client-chat message to go out later instead of only sending it immediately. Limited rollout.

items.text: CRM import improvements — preview & results screens
items.desc: CRM import now shows a preview and a results screen, so you can see what's about to happen before confirming.

items.text: CRM import improvements — precise import errors & warnings
items.desc: Import errors and warnings are now specific instead of generic, so it's easier to tell what actually needs fixing.

items.text: Password updates were failing across all environments
items.desc: A backend transaction issue was causing password changes to fail. Fixed — password updates work reliably again.

items.text: Client login: email OTP fails after portal access is re-granted
items.desc: Clients whose portal access was removed and then re-granted were getting stuck at the one-time-code login step. Fixed.

items.text: Some Stripe-connected accounts had payments unexpectedly break
items.desc: A reconciliation issue could disconnect otherwise-healthy Stripe payment connections, causing card payments to fail. Fixed.

items.text: Client chat was slow to send in long-running threads
items.desc: Sending a message in a client chat with a lot of history could take several seconds. Fixed — send times are back to normal regardless of thread length.

items.text: Recurring invoices could error under a rare timing conflict
items.desc: If a recurring invoice was being signed at the exact moment it renewed, it could throw an error. Fixed.

items.text: Accounts: Pending Activation list only exported first 30-35 rows
items.desc: Printing or exporting the Pending Activation list was cutting off partway through instead of including everyone. Now exports the full list.

items.text: Juno: documents stuck in "Sending" after a worker failure
items.desc: Some documents sent through Juno could get stuck showing "Sending" forever if a background job failed. Fixed.

items.text: Workflow: pipeline cloning duplicated automation templates
items.desc: Cloning a pipeline was creating duplicate copies of linked automation templates instead of reusing the original. Now reuses them correctly.

poll.label: Manage & charge payment methods for clients
